title: achillea clavennae var. capitata
intro: Achillea clavennae var. capitata, also known as Yarrow, is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the Asteraceae family. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Yarrow include reducing menstrual cramps, improving respiratory health, and acting as a natural insect repellent. Herbal preparations of Yarrow can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and can also be used topically as a poultice or oil to accelerate wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
